problem.title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
problem.description: Given a 2D matrix of integers, handle multiple queries of the form sumRegion(row1, col1, row2, col2), which returns the sum of all elements inside the rectangle defined by its upper-left corner (row1, col1) and lower-right corner (row2, col2). The matrix does not change after initialization.
assumptions: 0 <= row1, col1, row2, col2 < matrix.length / matrix[0].length
At most 10^4 calls to sumRegion will be made

example.explanation: sumRegion(2,1,4,3) sums elements in rows 2-4 and columns 1-3: 2+0+1 + 1+0+1 + 0+3+0 = 8. sumRegion(1,1,2,2) sums a 2x2 block: 6+3+2+0 = 11. sumRegion(1,2,2,4) sums a 2x3 block: 3+2+1+0+1+5 = 12.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Brute Force (Nested Loop per Query) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: For each sumRegion query, iterate through every element within the rectangle defined by (row1, col1) to (row2, col2) and sum them up directly. Simple to implement but expensive for repeated queries because each call is O(m*n) in the worst case where m and n are the rectangle dimensions.

complexity.time: O(1) constructor, O(m*n) per sumRegion call where m and n are the rectangle's row and column spans
complexity.space: O(1) extra space (just stores reference to the input matrix)

example_walkthrough.text:
For sumRegion(2,1,4,3) on the example matrix:
Row 2: matrix[2][1]+matrix[2][2]+matrix[2][3] = 2+0+1 = 3
Row 3: matrix[3][1]+matrix[3][2]+matrix[3][3] = 1+0+1 = 2
Row 4: matrix[4][1]+matrix[4][2]+matrix[4][3] = 0+3+0 = 3
Total = 3+2+3 = 8. With 10^4 queries on a 200x200 matrix, this becomes very slow.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – 2D Prefix Sum (Optimal O(1) Query) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Precompute a 2D prefix sum table during initialization where prefix[i][j] represents the sum of all elements in the sub-matrix from (0,0) to (i-1,j-1). The prefix table is sized (m+1) x (n+1) with an extra row and column of zeros to simplify boundary handling. For any query (row1, col1, row2, col2), the answer can be computed in O(1) using the inclusion-exclusion formula: prefix[row2+1][col2+1] - prefix[row1][col2+1] - prefix[row2+1][col1] + prefix[row1][col1]. The last term adds back the top-left rectangle that was subtracted twice.

complexity.time: O(m*n) for constructor preprocessing, O(1) per sumRegion query
complexity.space: O(m*n) for the prefix sum table

example_walkthrough.text:
Building prefix for matrix = [[3,0,1],[5,6,3],[1,2,0]]:
prefix[1][1] = 3, prefix[1][2] = 3, prefix[1][3] = 4
prefix[2][1] = 8, prefix[2][2] = 14, prefix[2][3] = 18
prefix[3][1] = 9, prefix[3][2] = 17, prefix[3][3] = 21

Query sumRegion(1,1,2,2):
= prefix[3][3] - prefix[1][3] - prefix[3][1] + prefix[1][1]
= 21 - 4 - 9 + 3 = 11 ✓

The formula removes the top strip and left strip from the full prefix, then adds back the top-left corner that was removed twice.
